name: CONTRACT-BUG-45
title: publish_settings overwrites settings.json clobbering cross-feature hook registrations
description:
publish_settings (lib/publish.py) does a full file copy from the feature's source settings.json to .claude/settings.json. When check_manifest_drift runs all feature manifests alphabetically, contract's publish_hook adds prompt-injector.py to PreToolUse in settings.json, then rabbit-cage's publish_settings overwrites settings.json from its source (which has no prompt-injector entry), dropping the registration. Rabbit-cage then re-adds its own hooks via publish_hook calls, but contract's hook is lost.

Net effect: every Stop event fires the 'Surface drift detected - rebuilt: contract, rabbit-cage' alert indefinitely because the drift check loop re-adds prompt-injector (contract pass), then clobbers it (rabbit-cage publish_settings), then can never reach a stable state.

Root cause: publish_settings must MERGE the hooks section rather than overwrite it entirely. The hooks section of .claude/settings.json is the shared registration surface for all features; a simple file-copy from one feature's source destroys other features' hook registrations.

Fix: modify publish_settings in lib/publish.py to:
1. Read the existing .claude/settings.json
2. Load the source settings.json from the feature
3. Merge: copy all non-hooks fields from source, then for the hooks section append any hook entries from source that are not already present (matching on event + command), preserving existing entries from other features
4. Write back the merged result

Idempotency holds as long as the merge check uses the same command-equality logic as publish_hook (exact command string match).

Observed during CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1 implementation: the prompt-injector PreToolUse hook (contract Inv 55, landed in Phase A.2/A.3) never reaches stable deployment because publish_settings clobbers it on every Stop cycle.
